title: rubus serpens subsp. chlorostachys
intro: Rubus serpens subsp. chlorostachys, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan black raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus serpens species, a type of bramble or raspberry native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's berries, leaves, and roots have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Rubus serpens subsp. chlorostachys include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves, roots, or berries in hot water or a solvent, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
